articles.title: 'They hate us and we hate them': England-Argentina semi comes with extra feeling

children.content: It is one of the biggest rivalries in men's World Cup history, and while the teams may downplay the history, England and Argentina fans are ready for another iconic showdown.

image.altText: A group of male Argentina fans jump and celebrate

articles.title: Why the England-Argentina rivalry is one of the ugliest in football

children.content: England's World Cup semifinal clash against Argentina is the latest chapter in a sporting rivalry that dates back 60 years to an ill-tempered quarterfinal, featuring cheating, violence and redemption.

image.altText: Diego Maradona screams

articles.title: France falters at first real hurdle as superstars fail to shine

children.content: France's 2026 World Cup campaign is ended by a poor 2-0 defeat to Spain, as the much-hyped collection of star forwards fail to turn up for the biggest game yet.

image.altText: Desire Doue pulls his jersey over his face in disappointment

children.content: England and Argentina rekindle one of the World Cup's most heated rivalries when they meet in the semifinal in Atlanta, with Spain awaiting the winner. Follow live.

items.title: Argentina claims late equaliser to pull England back in World Cup semifinal
Albanese rolls out a rule book and a red carpet for AI companies

children.content: If Australia is truly going to coax major AI companies to choose us rather than the other suitor nations with similar ambitions, some tough decisions need to be made soon.

image.altText: AlbaneseUYSYDwrap

items.title: PM backs copyright as artists seek voice on AI

children.content: The creative sector hopes there is a space to contribute to the Office of AI, given its content powers a lot of these tools.

image.altText: A woman in black sits in front of an ARIA sign.

items.title: Q-Day is coming and it might break the entire internet

children.content: The countdown is on to Q-Day, when an algorithm written more than 30 years ago could run on a quantum computer and compromise the security of data on the internet. How are we preparing?

image.altText: hand hovering over a laptop keyboard that's lit up in blue, in front of a blurry city background.

items.title: Gender quota debate revived in Liberals' soul-searching mission

children.content: Liberal Party HQ has admitted it is "in the political fight of our lives" as it canvasses ideas, including reviving gender quotas, to take the party forward.

image.altText: A man in a blue suit stares at the camera, with two people standing behind him

items.title: US launches strikes on key Iranian island amid renewed threats

children.content: US Central Command said it launched a 90-minute attack targeting Iran's strategic Greater Tunb Island.

articles.title: Carrying 400kg warheads, 'remote-controlled speedboats' sail into battle

children.content: The United States is using one-way drone boats in combat against Iran for the first time in its military history. A defence expert says America is catching on to this "cheaper and more effective" tactic employed by Iran and Ukraine.

image.altText: The Corsair drone boat appears to be turning in the sea.

articles.title: Trump beats his own record for strategic backflips on Iran

children.content: Donald Trump's actions this week have only confirmed he has run out of options and is now engaged in a simple war of wills, and calculations on who will run out of munitions first, with an enemy that he has underestimated from the start.

image.altText: A close-up of a man with blonde hair and a navy suit

articles.title: Global reserves that spared the worst of the oil shock are spent, IMF says

children.content: The International Monetary Fund says the global economy was spared the worst of the oil price shock because of large reserves. Now they are running out.

image.altText: Rows of white oil tanks are seen outdoors in front of a refinery.

items.title: Trump's tariffs turn liability as businesses claw back $110 billion

children.content: Businesses globally are clawing back tens of billions of dollars paid to United States customs after Donald Trump's so-called liberation day tariffs were deemed unlawful.

image.altText: a man in a warehouse smiling

items.title: Why Australians shouldn't panic about fuel prices as war in Iran escalates again

children.content: With the price of oil rising and the fuel excise cut due to end, drivers are facing twin threats when it comes to the price of fuel, but experts say Australia is in a much better position compared with when the Middle East conflict began.

image.altText: A hand holding a petrol pump hose, filling a car with fuel.

children.content: Train operators in rural Canada were trapped by raging wildfires, as nearby communities were evacuated.

items.title: Original architect of NDIS says scheme is 'failing' people it was set up to help

items.title: Electoral boundary shakeup leaves politicians with a big decision

children.content: Some notable politicians are among the 114,000 Tasmanians displaced by electoral boundary changes, and now have a difficult decision to make — stick with the electorate that voted them in, or move with the communities they've long been aligned with and live in.

items.title: Former employees of charity speak out about $150k in unpaid super

children.content: Former staff of charity PAWS Darwin are speaking out about being short-changed on their superannuation to the tune of $150,000. Under-18 staff also say the super accounts PAWS promised to create for them never existed.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a white woman, with blonde hair tied back, black singlet top, sitting among greenery.

items.title: GP clinic network hit by major cyber breach

children.content: Partnered Health, which operates more than 50 general practice and skin cancer clinics across Australia, has identified more than a dozen clinics where it fears patient information was taken.

items.title: Caring for the world's fastest falcon means preparing for bird flu

children.content: Peregrine falcons and wedge-tailed eagles could be among the first native species to contract a strain of bird flu that has recently arrived in Australia.

items.title: Moira Deeming drops court bid against party after last-ditch plea to retain candidacy
